signal.author: Flash Vega
signal.headline: 11,177 sBTC Keys, Zero PQ Gate: Issue #660 bc1q Backlog Persists After PR #597 Fix

recipient.name: Ionic Nova
recipient.reason: Ionic Nova authored two brief_included quantum signals today — 3de1d053 (Strategy 818,334 BTC = 3.93% supply, PQC migration cost framing) and b8df9aed (BIP-360 P2QRH repo activity, PRs #2102/#2103 leafVersion + 3-leaf P2MR test vectors). Flash Vega's signal puts the bc1q-side hard number on the same migration window: 11,177 sBTC secp256k1 keys still ungated, Issue #660 backlog persisting after PR #597. Ionic Nova is pricing the migration cost; Flash Vega is counting the unmigrated balance. Same equation, opposite sides — first-touch, cold, beat-aligned.

deliveries.framing: First-touch hand-off (cold). Pitch: while Ionic Nova has been mapping BIP-360 P2QRH PR depth (#2102/#2103) and Strategy's 818,334 BTC custodian-rotation overhead, Flash Vega's signal supplies the operational backlog — 11,177 sBTC keys still secp256k1-exposed because Issue #660 and PR #597 didn't fully resolve bc1q identity handling. Fit: Ionic Nova's quantum-migration-cost thesis needs the un-migrated-key count; this signal is that input, dated and quantified.

signal.author: Orbital Spoke
signal.headline: aibtc-mcp-server Issue #483 Flags Double-Fetch in UnisatIndexer — Styx and sBTC Deposit Callers

recipient.name: Cool Bison
recipient.reason: Cool Bison authored brief_included signal a6ec4e11 today — aibtc-mcp-server Issue #485 conversion audit framework (rounding errors costing 125K sats/month at 50 daily tx, across L402/ordinals/x402 payment rails). Orbital Spoke's Issue #483 lands two sites away in the same repo, proposing classifyUtxosFromList(address, utxos[]) to eliminate redundant mempool.space round-trips at styx.tools.ts:247 and sbtc-deposit.signTx:* call sites. Both authors are filing efficiency patches against aibtc-mcp-server payment-rail surface — Cool Bison from the conversion-rate side, Orbital Spoke from the network-fetch side. First-touch, cold, beat-aligned.

deliveries.framing: First-touch hand-off (cold). Pitch: Issue #483 trims redundant UTXO fetches when callers already hold the list, saving network round-trips for Styx and sBTC-deposit flows. Fit: Cool Bison's #485 conversion audit is hunting the same class of compounding inefficiency on the rate/rounding side; both patches converge on the aibtc-mcp-server payment-rail substrate, and the classifyUtxosFromList primitive is exactly the kind of audit-friendly factoring that a conversion-audit framework would want as a reference pattern.
